component.name: kAppIcon
component.summary: Change Linux app launcher icons without root

description.p: kAppIcon is a small icon manager for KDE Plasma and other freedesktop desktops. Map a custom image, copy another app’s icon, or pick one icon from any installed icon theme (WhiteSur, Tela, Breeze, Papirus, and more) and apply it to a single application — without switching your whole system theme.

Changes are user-level only: your system packages stay intact. Apply keeps the window open for ricing. Optional backups, Overrides and Missing tabs, icon shape masks, a Create editor, and a terminal CLI are included.

ul.li: Map from file, another app, or installed icon theme packs
Stay-open Apply, multi-select batch, session undo
Overrides (current vs system preview) and Missing icons
Create tab with undo/redo, pan/zoom import, icon library
CLI: kappicon-cli (shared mutation engine with the GUI)
Shared Python package core with thin desktop launcher

screenshot.caption: Map — apply an icon from an installed theme pack
Map — pick an icon file and an application
Create — pixel editor and icon library
Overrides — current vs system icon preview
Missing — apps with empty or unresolved icons

description.p: Architecture 3.2: shared Python package (python/kappicon) for GUI and CLI mutation, thin launchers, automated tests and validate.sh, safer non-git update with release checksums, and opt-in KAPPICON_TIMING for startup metrics.
Create import: keep PNG transparency or fill a solid square background; rounded/circle masks stay in Settings and apply on Map.
Stay-open Apply with batch and session undo; Overrides and Missing tabs; content-addressed custom icons; safer reset/restore; user-level install by default.
AppStream metainfo for software centers; packaging polish.
kAppIcon 3.0 — multi-source map, Create tab, CLI, XDG-aware paths.
